summary:
Claim: The most common cause of recurrent laryngeal nerve palsy is chronic compression by a growing mediastinal mass.  

Evidence: - Thyroid surgery causes one-time nerve injury with permanent paralysis but does not represent true recurrence due to absence of repeated events. - Mediastinal tumors grow gradually, leading to progressive nerve compression and symptomatic relapse over time, matching clinical definitions of "recurrent" palsy.  

Answer: C
